articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Dutch thrash Iceland 4-0 in warmup but De Jong out of Euros
article.description: Netherlands' delight with an emphatic 4-0 win over Iceland in their last warmup match on Monday before heading to the Euros turned to despair when it was announced that playmaker Frenkie de Jong would not be going to the tournament in Germany.
article.linkText: Dutch thrash Iceland 4-0 in Euros warmup

images.name: How a joke 36 years ago turned a whole neighbourhood orange
images.caption: A neighbourhood in the Netherlands gets ready for Euro 2024 the same way it does for every football competition -- by covering the town in orange decorations.

article.story:
Netherlands' delight with an emphatic 4-0 win over Iceland in their last warmup match on Monday before heading to the Euros turned to despair when it was announced that playmaker Frenkie de Jong would not be going to the tournament in Germany.

De Jong has been battling to be fit in time after suffering several ankle injuries this season playing for Barcelona.

He tried training for the first time with his team mates on Sunday, after a week of individual work, but following examinations on Monday doctors said he would not be ready in time.

That put a damper on Dutch spirits despite the impressive scoreline at the Feijenoord Stadium.

De Jong's diagnosis was made public immediately after the game but coach Ronald Koeman said he knew about it some four hours before kickoff and had told the players.

"Tests have shown that he cannot yet do what he should be able to do. He gets a reaction from his ankle every time after training," Koeman told Dutch television.

"Then you have to conclude that he will not be 100% fit for the next three weeks. I already thought that playing against Poland (in the Group D opener) would be difficult."

De Jong posted a reaction on social media. "I am sad and disappointed that I will not make it to the European Championship," said the 26-year-old.

"We have done everything we can in recent weeks, but unfortunately my ankle needs more time.

"It is a dream and the greatest honor to represent your country at a final tournament, to wear the orange shirt, sing the national anthem and feel the support of the entire country. But now I, like the entire Orange legion, will cheer our team on from the sidelines. Let's go boys."

Earlier the Dutch made sure of a good send off in their last warm-up game as Xavi Simons finally scored for his country, in his 14th international, to put the Dutch 1-0 up at halftime.

Virgil van Dijk got on the scoresheet for a second successive game before Donyell Malen added the third.

Wout Weghorst netted the fourth on the stroke of full time as the Dutch prepare to face Poland on Sunday in Hamburg followed by France and Austria in the group stage at the finals. (Writing by Mark Gleeson; Editing by Ken Ferris)

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Netherlands 1, Iceland 0. Xavi Simons (Netherlands) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Denzel Dumfries with a headed pass.
Substitution, Iceland. Stefán Thórdarson replaces Arnór Ingvi Traustason.
Goal!  Netherlands 2, Iceland 0. Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) header from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Nathan Aké   following a corner.
Substitution, Iceland. Ísak Bergmann Jóhannesson replaces Jón Thorsteinsson.
Substitution, Iceland. Arnór Sigurdsson replaces Mikael Anderson.
Substitution, Netherlands. Micky van de Ven replaces Nathan Aké.
Substitution, Netherlands. Georginio Wijnaldum replaces Jerdy Schouten.
Substitution, Netherlands. Donyell Malen replaces Cody Gakpo.
Substitution, Netherlands. Lutsharel Geertruida replaces Denzel Dumfries.
Substitution, Netherlands. Jeremie Frimpong replaces Xavi Simons.
Goal!  Netherlands 3, Iceland 0. Donyell Malen (Netherlands) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Memphis Depay.
Substitution, Netherlands. Wout Weghorst replaces Memphis Depay.
Substitution, Iceland. Brynjar Ingi Bjarnason replaces Sverrir Ingason.
Substitution, Iceland. Kristian Hlynsson replaces Hákon Haraldsson.
Goal!  Netherlands 4, Iceland 0. Wout Weghorst (Netherlands) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Donyell Malen.Goal awarded following VAR Review.
